ground_truth.title: Best Long-Distance & Cross-Country Movers 2026
ground_truth.author: Rebecca Edwards
ground_truth.main_content: We analyzed 75+ long-distance movers and 10,000+ reviews to match you with the best — and cheapest — options for your move. Use our Find-a-Mover tool to get a moving estimate in minutes. We’ve spent nearly a decade researching and testing long-distance movers to find companies that deliver fair pricing, reliable service, and fewer moving-day surprises. Allied Van Lines is our top overall pick, with strong alternatives like Colonial Van Lines, United Van Lines, American Van Lines, and Mayzlin for different budgets and priorities. Want a faster answer? Use our Find-a-Mover tool to compare personalized estimates from the movers that best fit your move. The best long-distance movers of 2026: 1. Allied Van Lines: Best overall interstate mover, 2. Colonial Van Lines: Best-trained movers, 3. American Van Lines: Best value, 4. United Van Lines: No deposit required, 5. Mayzlin: Best discounts. What are the cheapest days to hire movers? In general, weekdays are the cheapest days to hire movers. Weekends are typically the busiest, so if you're able to schedule your move during the week, you can often get a cheaper rate. Compare top-rated long-distance movers and find the best fit for your move. Pick the right mover for your priorities: Choose Colonial for highly trained movers, Choose American for overall value, Choose United or Allied if you want no deposit, Choose Mayzlin for discounts and price matching. Since pricing and availability depend on your move details, the fastest way to narrow this down is to compare estimates directly. How to choose a long-distance mover: Cost: Costs for a professional mover can vary from $1,200 up to $29,000, depending distance, home sizes, and services. Beware of extremely cheap quotes that seem too good to be true — they usually are. Check out our Moving Cost guide to see average prices by distance and type of move, then use our Find-a-Mover tool to compare real estimates based on your route and home size. Affordable deposit: Most movers charge a deposit of 10%–50% of the total cost. to minimize risk, pick a mover with lower or no deposits. And before you book, confirm refund and cancellation policies. Customer service: Good movers communicate clearly before, during, and after your move. If you're moving on the weekend, look for a company with 24/7 customer support. Timelines: Moving timelines vary based on the logistics of your move. If you have a firm move-in or move-out date, ask if guaranteed delivery is available for your route (it may cost extra). Binding estimates: Binding quotes guarantee that your price won’t change after signing on the dotted line — as long as you don’t have any inventory changes. This is key to avoiding surprise fees on moving day. Carriers vs. brokers: Carriers use their own moving fleets and in-house movers, while brokers work with partner moving companies. Both are legit ways to move, but check licensing, insurance, and reputation before you sign a contract or pay a deposit. Perks: It's called full-service moving for a reason. Look for perks like real-time GPS tracking, supplemental insurance, free storage, and special discounts. What to expect during a long-distance moving quote: Once you've decided to hire a mover, you've only got a few steps left to get a quote for your move. Here's how it works: 1. Share your move details: Movers need to know about your route, timing, and home size to give you an accurate estimate. 2. Narrow down your options: Pick the movers you want a quote from, based on your top priorities (e.g., deposits, services, discounts, etc.). 3. Review and compare options: Your selected movers will follow up with details about their services and costs. 4. Finalize your quote: After you've picked the estimate you like, a moving coordinator will work with you to help you take inventory for your move, confirm dates, discuss extras like packing or storage, and lock in your price. Tip: Get more quotes faster: Use our Find-a-Mover tool to complete these steps in one place (and in under one minute!) and compare estimates from vetted and Move.org-endorsed movers faster! For a deeper dive, check out What Goes Into a Moving Estimate? and How to Do a Home Inventory. The best cross-country moving company reviews: 1. Allied Van Lines: Best overall long-distance moving company. Allied Van Lines stands out for its convenience, offering virtual in-home estimates and being one of the few moving companies that doesn’t require a deposit. Over the past 15 years, Allied has helped approximately 750,000 families move, earning a spot on our list of best long-distance moving companies every year since 2016.
